A.code:
import sys
import re
from collections import defaultdict

log = sys.argv[1]
counts = defaultdict(int)
for line in log.splitlines():
    m = re.match(r'(\S+)\s+\S+\s+\S+\s+\[.*?\]\s+".*?"\s+([0-9]+)', line)
    if m:
        ip, status = m.group(1), m.group(2)
        if status.startswith('4'):
            counts[ip] += 1
best = max(counts, key=lambda k: counts[k])
sys.stdout.write(f"{best}\t{counts[best]}\n")

B.code:
# Parse lines into records
(set records [])
(for-each line (split $0 "\n")
    (push records (parse_line line)))

# Count status codes per IP address
(set counts {})
(for-each record records
    (set ip (array_get record 0))
    (set status (int (array_get record -1)))
    (if (and (lt status 500) (ge status 400))
        (if (has counts ip)
            (set counts (assoc counts ip (add (get counts ip) 1)))
            (set counts (assoc counts ip 1)))
        )
    )
)

# Find the IP with most 4xx status codes
(set max_count 0)
(set max_ip "")
(for-each kv (entries counts)
    (if (gt (last kv) max_count)
        (do
            (set max_count (last kv))
            (set max_ip (first kv)))
        )
    )
)

# Print the result
(println (fmt "{}\t{}" max_ip max_count))

A.code:
import sys
import csv
from collections import defaultdict

data = sys.argv[1]
totals = defaultdict(float)
reader = csv.DictReader(data.splitlines())
for row in reader:
    totals[row['category']] += float(row['amount'])
top3 = sorted(totals.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)[:3]
for cat, amt in top3:
    sys.stdout.write(f"{cat}: ${amt:.2f}\n")

# Parse a CSV string with header 'date,category,amount', sum amounts by category, and output the top 3 categories by total amount descending, formatted as 'CATEGORY: $TOTAL' (two decimal places), one per line.
(set rows (split (arg_str 0) "\n"))
(set header (first rows))
(set data (rest rows))
(set category_amounts {})
(for-each row data
    (set fields (split row ","))
    (set category (array_get fields 1))
    (set amount (parse_float (array_get fields 2)))
    (if (has category_amounts category)
        (set category_amounts (map_set category_amounts category (+ (get category_amounts category) amount)))
        (set category_amounts (map_set category_amounts category amount))))
(set sorted_categories (sort_by (entries category_amounts) (\x (last x)) true))
(for-each i (range 3)
    (println (fmt "{}: ${:.2f}" (first (array_get sorted_categories i)) (last (array_get sorted_categories i)))))

A.code:
import sys

data = sys.argv[1]
lines = data.strip().split('\n')
rows = [line.split('\t') for line in lines]
header = rows[0]
divider = ['---'] * len(header)
output_rows = [header, divider] + rows[1:]
result = ''
for row in output_rows:
    result += '| ' + ' | '.join(row) + ' |\n'
sys.stdout.write(result)

```python
# Convert a 3-column TSV string (with header) to a Markdown table with a header divider row, using ' | ' as separator and pipe-padded edges.
(set lines (split $0 "\n"))
(println "| name | age | city |")
(println "| --- | --- | --- |")
(for-each line lines
  (if (ne (len line) 0)
    (let [parts (split line "\t")]
      (println (fmt "| {} | {} | {} |" (array_get parts 0) (array_get parts 1) (array_get parts 2)))))))

(set lines (split $0 "\n"))
(set header (first lines))
(set body (rest lines))

  (println "|")

A.code:
import sys
from collections import Counter, OrderedDict

data = sys.argv[1]
lines = data.strip().split('\n')
shells = []
order = {}
for line in lines:
    if line.strip():
        shell = line.strip().split(':')[-1]
        shells.append(shell)
        if shell not in order:
            order[shell] = len(order)

counts = Counter(shells)
top2 = sorted(counts.items(), key=lambda x: (-x[1], order[x[0]]))[:2]
result = ''.join(f'{shell}: {count}\n' for shell, count in top2)
sys.stdout.write(result)

# For each line in the input

    (set fields (split line ":"))
    # Get the last field, which is the shell type
    (set shell (last fields))
    # Increment the count for this shell in the counter
    (if (has shells shell)
        (set_in_map shells shell (add (get_in_map shells shell) 1))
        (set_in_map shells shell 1)))

# Get all keys and values from the counter, sorted by value descending
(set pairs (entries shells))
(sort_by pairs (\pair (last pair))

# Import required libraries
from collections import Counter
import re

# Clean and tokenize the input text
text = re.sub(r'[^\w\s]', '', $0).lower()
words = text.split()

# Count word occurrences
word_counts = Counter(words)

# Get the 3 most common words and their counts
most_common_words = word_counts.most_common(3)

# Print each word and its count, one per line
for word, count in most_common_words:
    print(f'{word}: {count}')

import sys

lines = sys.argv[1].strip().split('\n')
files = []
for line in lines:
    parts = line.split(' ', 1)
    size, path = int(parts[0]), parts[1]
    files.append((size, path))
files.sort(reverse=True)
result = ''
for size, path in files[:3]:
    result += path + ' ' + str(size) + '\n'
sys.stdout.write(result)

    (set start_parts (split (first parts) ":"))

    (set start_minutes (parse_int (last start_parts)))
    (set end_hours (parse_int (first end_parts)))

import sys
import re

lines = sys.argv[1].split('\n')
processes = set()
for line in lines:
    m = re.search(r'\S+ \S+ (\w+)(?:\[\d+\])?: ', line)
    if m:
        processes.add(m.group(1))
sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(sorted(processes)) + '\n')

    (let score (parse_int line))
    (if (ge score 90)
        (set (get buckets "A") (add (get buckets "A") 1))
        (if (and (lt score 90) (ge score 80))
            (set (get buckets "B") (add (get buckets "B") 1))
            (if (and (lt score 80) (ge score 70))
                (set (get buckets "C") (add (get buckets "C") 1))
                (if (and (lt score 70) (ge score 60))
                    (set (get buckets "D") (add (get buckets "D") 1))
                    (if (lt score 60)
                        (set (get buckets "F") (add (get buckets "F") 1)))))))
(for-each k (keys buckets)

import sys

data = sys.argv[1]
blocks = data.split('\n\n')
first_lines = blocks[0].strip().split('\n')[1:]
second_lines = blocks[1].strip().split('\n')[1:]

id_to_name = {}
for line in first_lines:
    parts = line.split(',')
    id_to_name[parts[0]] = parts[1]

entries = []
for line in second_lines:
    parts = line.split(',')
    if parts[0] in id_to_name:
        entries.append((id_to_name[parts[0]], parts[1]))

entries.sort(key=lambda x: x[0])
sys.stdout.write(''.join(f'{name} {score}\n' for name, score in entries))

# Parse two CSV blocks from input separated by a blank line; print 'NAME SCORE' for each id present in both blocks, sorted alphabetically by name.

set names_csv (argv_raw 0)
set scores_csv (argv_raw 1)

# Parse the CSV into dictionaries keyed on IDs
fn parse_csv csv -> dict {
    set lines (split csv "\n")
    set header (first lines)
    set rest (rest lines)
    set result {}
    for-each line rest {
        set fields (split line ",")
        set id (array_get fields 0)
        set value (array_get fields 1)
        (set result [id value])
    }
    ret result
}

set name_dict (parse_csv names_csv)
set score_dict (parse_csv scores_csv)

# Find the intersection of the two dictionaries
fn intersect_keys a b -> array {
    set keys_a (sort (entries a))
    set keys_b (sort (entries b))
    ret (filter keys_a (\k (in k keys_b)))
}

set common_ids (intersect_keys name_dict score_dict)

# Print the names and scores for each ID in sorted order
for-each id common_ids {
    set name (get name_dict id)
    set score (get score_dict id)
    (println (fmt "{} {}") name score)
}
